input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-16T02:20:06.904Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which has been removed. Let me look at their test file to understand the current code and provide the replacement.

data.content: MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is **`Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()`** (for exact type matching) or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` (which also matches derived types). I'll update your tests and bump the package version.
data.reasoningText:
MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` which existed since v2). Let me update the test file to use `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` which is the modern MSTest v4 approach.

Let me also update the package version to v4.

toolCalls.name: edit

index 0000000..0000000 100644

@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@
   </PropertyGroup>
 
   <ItemGroup>
-    <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
+    <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
 </Project>
